zh.Rotherfield, Sussex,, England: 英國英格蘭素可洛瑟費爾德
zh.Rotherfield, Wealden District, East Suss: 英國英格蘭易亞斯特 素可洛瑟費爾德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert de Grey於從1349年1月9日到1350年1月8日出生在英國英格蘭素可洛瑟費爾德時，他的父親John Grey11347歲，母親Margaret de Burghersh18歲。他於1378年3月28日與Elizabeth de la Plaunche在英國英格蘭奧可福爾結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1387年1月14日在英國英格蘭易亞斯特 素可洛瑟費爾德，埋葬在英國英格蘭易亞斯特 素可洛瑟費爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Robert de Grey于从1349年1月9日到1350年1月8日出生在英国英格兰素可洛瑟費爾德时，他的父亲John Grey11347岁，母亲Margaret de Burghersh18岁。他于1378年3月28日与Elizabeth de la Plaunche在英国英格兰奧可福爾结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1387年1月14日在英国英格兰易亞斯特 素可洛瑟費爾德，埋葬在英国英格兰易亞斯特 素可洛瑟費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Grey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

variants.name: Gray
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Greiwe
meanings: German: possibly a nickname from Middle High German greibe ‘sharp, tangy, dour’. Compare Grey .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert de Grey was born from 9 January 1349 to 8 January 1350, in Rotherfield, Sussex, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Grey, was 11347 and his mother, Margaret de Burghersh, was 18. He married Elizabeth de la Plaunche on 28 March 1378, in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 14 January 1387, in Rotherfield, East Sussex, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Rotherfield, East Sussex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert de Grey est né du 9 janvier 1349 au 8 janvier 1350, à Rotherfield, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Grey, avait 11347 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Burghersh, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth de la Plaunche le 28 mars 1378, à Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé le 14 janvier 1387, à Rotherfield, East Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Rotherfield, East Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de Greyがから1349年1月9日まで1350年1月8日にイギリスイギリススセックスロスエルフィールドで生まれた時，父John Greyは11347歳，母Margaret de Burghershは18歳であった。1378年3月28日にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアでElizabeth de la Plauncheと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1387年1月14日にイギリスイギリスエアスト スセックスロスエルフィールドで死去し，イギリスイギリスエアスト スセックスロスエルフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de Grey(이)가 1349년 1월 9일부터 1350년 1월 8일까지 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 로더필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Grey(은)는 11347세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret de Burghersh(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1378년 3월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르에서 Elizabeth de la Plaunche(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1387년 1월 14일 영국 잉글랜드 이스트 수세스 로더필드에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 이스트 수세스 로더필드에 묻혔다.

ru.1349–14 January 1387: с 9 января 1349 по 8 января 1350–14 января 1387 г.
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert de Grey родился с 9 января 1349 по 8 января 1350 в местности ротирфиелд, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Grey было 11347 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Burghersh, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth de la Plaunche 28 марта 1378 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 14 января 1387 г. в местности ротирфиелд, эаст суссекс, Англия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности ротирфиелд, эаст суссекс, Англия, Великобритания.
